title: send signal to pidfd: Permission denied
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**
We use AWS SSM to run/restart podman container. This execution is done using root account. The first time shell script is executed, podman containers are started properly. Next time, when we send AWS SSM command to restart containers (podman stop, podman rm, podman run) we get issue with podman stop. Below is a bash snippet that we use to stop running containers
```
   podman ps -q | xargs -r podman stop

   sleep 10

   podman ps -a -q | xargs -r podman rm -f
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Using AWS SSM command, start one or more containers with `podman run`

2. Using AWS SSM command, stop containers, remove them, and start them again

**Describe the results you received:**
```
time="2022-02-23T23:36:28Z" level=info msg="podman filtering at log level debug"

time="2022-02-23T23:36:28Z" level=debug msg="Called stop.PersistentPreRunE(podman stop --log-level=DEBUG 50cbc4d68f59 9842b4dbbd11)"

Error: timed out waiting for file /run/libpod/exits/9842b4dbbd1118fc4910a29a8ff7d38e97ca91839ac807669af7c399c0fe52a5: internal libpod error

Error: timed out waiting for file /run/libpod/exits/50cbc4d68f5907d613481b9b1ab5b8b5315bd2ea505178e6f2ff39caeb9a74af: internal libpod error

failed to run commands: exit status 123
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
Containers to stop properly.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AWS EC2 instance with Ubuntu: Linux ip-10-60-2-134 5.11.0-1022-aws #23~20.04.1-Ubuntu SMP Mon Nov 15 14:03:19 UTC 2021 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

I cannot reproduce the issue when I use my own Ubuntu VM on VMware.

comments.body: Why are you using separate `podman stop` and `podman rm` commands? `podman rm -af` should suffice to get rid of every container on the system, running or not running, in a single command.

> Why are you using separate `podman stop` and `podman rm` commands? `podman rm -af` should suffice to get rid of every container on the system, running or not running, in a single command.

I didn't paste full command:
```
  podman ps 'label=<something>' -q | xargs -r podman stop

   sleep 10

   podman ps  'label=<something>'  -a -q | xargs -r podman rm -f
```

Also, same shell script is used for docker which doesn't seem to support -a for rm

Even using one command triggered issue:

```
failed to run commands: exit status 125
```

I am running into the same issue on Ubuntu 20.04, podman version 3.4.2:

```shell
sudo podman run --rm -it --name=sleep --detach ubuntu:20.04 sleep 1000
sudo podman kill --signal=KILL sleep

# output:
# 2022-03-22T01:43:27.000294649Z: send signal to pidfd: Permission denied
# Error: error sending signal to container b273117b259da38007effcbafcb9c7544d54fbf454a62c8a2bf86c044f498dfd: `/usr/bin/crun kill b273117b259da38007effcbafcb9c7544d54fbf454a62c8a2bf86c044f498dfd 9` failed: exit status 1
```

The same issue happens whether I use `sudo podman kill --signal=KILL` or `sudo podman rm -f`.

Running the output `crun` command directly (with sudo) also doesn't work:

```shell
sudo /usr/bin/crun kill b273117b259da38007effcbafcb9c7544d54fbf454a62c8a2bf86c044f498dfd 9
# output:
# 2022-03-22T01:43:34.000939261Z: send signal to pidfd: Permission denied
```

I ran into [this gist](https://gist.github.com/zenthangplus/4b2d534fa85031ae6c652ffe16ae5da2) which suggested it might be related to apparmor, and indeed it looks related to apparmor. I ran the following:

```shell
sudo systemctl disable apparmor.service --now
sudo aa-teardown

sudo podman run --rm -it --name=sleep --detach ubuntu:20.04 sleep 1000
sudo podman kill --signal=KILL sleep
```

And it works now.

I don't know why disabling apparmor fixes the problem though, and I would like to help figure out how to fix this without disabling apparmor. Happy to share more info about my system and/or try out various debugging commands if it would help at all.
we dont currently have any apparmor experts on the team.  where did you get podman from? if it was from ubuntu, can you create a launchpad with them and perhaps get some help?
this doesn't look like a podman issue, AppArmor should not block the `pidfd_send_signal` call
